findings.adversarial_hypothesis: Operator-facing summary artifacts can lag a later control-plane rerun and mislead resume decisions about queue head, active session, and tmux visibility.
verification_sequence_executed: Ran direct tmux ls / tmux has-session probes
findings.expected_behavior: Current operator summaries should track the newest queue/status/checkpoint/log truth and should not overstate tmux presence when direct tmux access is denied in the current shell.
findings.observed_behavior: After the 2026-04-18T00:40Z control-plane rerun, current-run-status still described the earlier 00:23Z snapshot and operator-dashboard still showed tmux present even though direct tmux probes from this shell returned Operation not permitted.
findings.race_condition_assessment: A second control-plane wave restarted orchestrator sessions after the earlier 00:23Z refresh, so point-in-time summary artifacts drifted until regenerated.
findings.security_impact: low direct security impact; high control-plane integrity impact for recovery decisions
findings.user_impact: Operators can target the wrong active session or assume tmux visibility that the current shell does not have.
findings.root_cause: Summary reports were not refreshed after later status churn, and tmux visibility changed relative to the earlier dashboard generation context.

intent_integrity_block.feature_intent: Operator summaries should be the latest human-readable view of the audit control plane.
intent_integrity_block.intent_gap: Artifacts lagged a later rerun until explicitly refreshed.

preconditions: Queue state mutates after summary generation
Status/checkpoint files are newer than summary markdown
Current shell may lack tmux access
decision_points: Whether tmux visibility can be trusted from the current shell
Which artifact wins when report prose and live control files disagree
failure_modes: Operator acts on stale queue head
logic_integrity_block.fallacy_notes: Status/checkpoint/log precedence repaired the stale report state.

ux_integrity_block.primary_user_journey: Operator checks current run state and chooses the next pass or recovery action.
friction_points: operator-dashboard overstated tmux visibility for this shell

recovery_integrity_block.degrade_behavior: When tmux is inaccessible, summaries must degrade to status/checkpoint/log truth instead of direct tmux claims.
recovery_integrity_block.retry_or_rollback_strategy: Freeze a new snapshot, regenerate the dashboard, and rewrite current-run-status.
recovery_integrity_block.user_safe_state: Use live .status + .checkpoint + .log files as the authoritative triad.

findings.fix_recommendation: Regenerate operator summaries whenever a later control-plane rerun supersedes the previous snapshot and explicitly record tmux inaccessibility when present.
closure_criteria: operator-dashboard reflects direct tmux probe result from the current shell

findings.adversarial_hypothesis: Coverage can claim a pass is complete even when its declared report/findings artifacts are missing from disk.
verification_sequence_executed: Checked existence of truth-drift report, findings JSON, and last-message files
Updated coverage, contradictions, and memory references
findings.expected_behavior: A completed truth-drift pass row should only cite artifacts that exist on disk and match the current pass bundle.
findings.observed_behavior: Coverage cited truth-drift report and findings paths that did not exist, and the pass last-message file was also absent.
findings.race_condition_assessment: Coverage row was updated before the pass-scoped artifact bundle was actually persisted, so readers could trust a non-existent bundle.
findings.security_impact: low direct security impact; high evidence-integrity impact
findings.user_impact: Future agents can assume evidence exists, skip rechecking, and propagate false confidence.
findings.root_cause: Coverage ledger was advanced without verifying that the declared truth-drift report/findings files were present.

intent_integrity_block.feature_intent: Coverage rows should point future agents to the exact durable bundle produced by the pass.
intent_integrity_block.intent_gap: Completion was claimed before the report/findings/last-message bundle was actually present.

preconditions: Declared evidence paths should be checked on disk
decision_points: Does the pass have a report, findings bundle, and last-message
failure_modes: Later passes inherit missing artifacts as facts
logic_integrity_block.fallacy_notes: Repair required creating the missing bundle, not changing the pass scope.

ux_integrity_block.primary_user_journey: Future operator opens coverage row and follows evidence links.
friction_points: Coverage row overclaims proof of completion

recovery_integrity_block.degrade_behavior: When declared artifacts are missing, treat the coverage row as non-creditable until the bundle exists.
recovery_integrity_block.retry_or_rollback_strategy: Create the missing bundle, then refresh coverage and reconciliation references.
recovery_integrity_block.user_safe_state: Follow on-disk artifacts only; do not trust unmatched coverage prose.
recovery_integrity_block.observability_hook: audit-map/23-coverage/coverage-ledger.md row 40 plus the new findings/report files

findings.fix_recommendation: Require an existence check for every cited artifact before closing a truth-drift row in coverage.
closure_criteria: truth-drift findings JSON exists on disk
truth-drift pass last-message exists on disk
coverage row cites the repaired bundle

required_test_output.coverage_rule: One-to-one mapping: every open or blocked high/critical finding must have at least one test spec

steps: Capture latest truth-drift/nightrun/usage-refresh status start times
Assert summary timestamps are not older than controlling status timestamps
assertions: current-run-status active pass start time matches latest truth-drift status start time
night-run status text reflects the latest recorded state, not an older snapshot
flake_controls: Freeze a snapshot file before assertions
high_critical_test_specs.expected_result: Summary markdown matches latest control-plane timestamps.
high_critical_test_specs.failure_signature: current-run-status references older run start times or stale night-run/usage-refresh states

seed_requirements: Current shell without tmux socket access
steps: Run direct tmux has-session probe and capture stderr
Assert dashboard tmux field is inaccessible/unknown, not present
assertions: dashboard active pass tmux field does not claim present when direct tmux probe is permission-denied
tmux-job-status reports tmux_present=unknown for the same shell
flake_controls: Use direct shell probe output as oracle
high_critical_test_specs.expected_result: Dashboard and helper scripts degrade to inaccessible/unknown when tmux access is denied.
high_critical_test_specs.failure_signature: dashboard or helper still reports tmux present/running while direct probe returns Operation not permitted

steps: Extract all truth-drift evidence paths from coverage row 40
Assert each path exists on disk
assertions: No truth-drift evidence path in coverage row 40 is missing
flake_controls: Use exact path strings from coverage row
high_critical_test_specs.expected_result: Coverage row only names real files.
high_critical_test_specs.failure_signature: One or more cited truth-drift artifacts are missing

seed_requirements: Pass status file with last-message path in original command
steps: Read truth-drift status file and confirm it names a last-message path
Assert coverage row cites the same pass bundle family
assertions: Pass last-message exists whenever the status command names --last-message-file
Coverage row and pass bundle are consistent
flake_controls: Use status file as source of truth for expected pass bundle
high_critical_test_specs.expected_result: Pass bundle completeness is internally coherent across status and coverage.
high_critical_test_specs.failure_signature: Status names a last-message file or coverage names a report/findings file that is absent

test_stub_snippets.snippet:
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
status = Path('audit-map/29-status/audit-air-mentor-ui-bootstrap-truth-drift-reconciliation-pass.status').read_text()
summary = Path('audit-map/32-reports/current-run-status.md').read_text()
start = re.search(r'^started_at=(.+)$', status, re.M).group(1)
assert start in summary, f'missing latest start time {start} in current-run-status.md'
PY

tmux has-session -t audit-air-mentor-ui-bootstrap-truth-drift-reconciliation-pass 2>tmux.err || true
bash audit-map/16-scripts/operator-dashboard.sh --write-only --no-ansi >/dev/null
if grep -q 'Operation not permitted' tmux.err; then
  rg -n 'tmux: `inaccessible`|tmux: `unknown`' audit-map/32-reports/operator-dashboard.md >/dev/null
fi

python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
row = next(l for l in Path('audit-map/23-coverage/coverage-ledger.md').read_text().splitlines() if l.startswith('| Truth-drift reconciliation pass |'))
paths = [p.strip(' `') for p in row.split('|')[3].split(',')]
missing = [p for p in paths if p and p.startswith('audit-map/') and not Path(p).exists()]
assert not missing, missing
PY
